text:
The European Union's principle of incorporating "essential elements" into its international trade agreements has been the object of a large amount of debate in the scientific community. In this context, this paper aims to take a critical look at EU trade policy from the perspective of the human rights dimension, especially when taking into account the trade human rights nexus. The latter is based on the principle of linking the EU's economic activities to the defence of human rights, an objective enshrined in the EU Treaty.

Paper Discussion: EU Trade Relations: Rule of Law Clause – an Effective Mechanism? On 29 June 2022, ECOS hosted a discussion on EU trade relations. This paper examines the rule of law clause in EU free trade agreements, building on an in-depth analysis of the ‘anatomy’ of the clause and its evolution. It takes a number of concerns and issues related to its application as its starting point, namely: (i) the non-uniform presence of the clause in all EU trade agreements; (ii) possible ambiguities in the wording of the clause and divergences in the scope of its provisions; and (iii) the difficulties in ensuring the clause’s verification and implementation. The paper concludes with some general remarks on the effectiveness and legitimacy of the rule of law clause, pointing out the potential adverse effects that the current application of the clause has on the EU’s credibility in implementing its rules-based trade agenda globally.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
